subject: Re: Exporting particles back to RELION after M refinement (MCore)
body:
Yunjie asked about the particle metadata format off list, copying my
responses to his questions below


1. M will generate a particle.star file and a tomogram.star file when I try
> to export particles.
>
Yes


> 2. In the tomogram.star file, the rlnTomoProjXYZW columns define how the
> tilt series are aligned and describe the transformation from 3D particle
> coordinates in the tomogram to 2D coordinates in the tilt series images.

3. In your recent RELION-5 tomography paper, it is mentioned that the
> transformation matrix R for tilt series alignment is defined by 5
> parameters: rlnTomoXTilt, YTilt, ZRot and XShiftAngst, YShiftAngst, which
> are different from the loopheaders in the tomogram.star generated by M. As
> I understand, rlnTomoProjXYZW columns in the tomogram.star from M
> correspond to the 1-4th rows of the transformation matrix R, is this
> correct? If so, how should I transfer the 3D coordinates to 2D with this
> 4*4 matrix? May you describe the formula?
>

There is some complexity here.

- the 4x4 matrix with rows rlnTomoProjX/Y/Z/W describes a rigid body
transformation of the sample in the microscope for a particular tilt image
- in RELION-4 the first two rows of this matrix were used directly to
calculate 2D positions in the tilt series, in pixels, from 3D positions in
the tomograms
[a11, a12, a13, a14] . [x, y, z, 1] = x'
[a21, a22, a23, a24] . [x, y, z, 1] = y'
a11 -> a14 is rlnTomoProjX, a21 -> a24 is rlnTomoProjY, . is the dot product
- for the above matrix to be used directly for these calculations it needs
to include where the tomogram origin is relative to the tomogram center and
where the tilt image origin is relative to the tilt image center, in a
given unit (pixels in this case) (c.f. RELION code
<https://github.com/3dem/relion/blob/4e57a4dbdbf643e9ee630f40e3a41d671eb325f2/src/jaz/tomography/tomogram.cpp#L20-L36>
).
- this has the unfortunate side effect of making the matrix specific to a
specific tomogram size and image pixel size, which is why we introduced the
XTilt, YTilt, ZRot, Xshift, YShift parametrisation (which is then used to
generate the matrices)
- once you have a particle series extracted with each image correctly
centered you no longer need to know the translation component which centers
your particle in the field of view - only the rotation which orients the
central fourier slice for that image
- warp extracts centered particle series and as such the translational part
is not needed for aligning these particles
- because of this we only populate the rotational component of the
rlnTomoProjX/Y/Z/W 4x4 matrix when generating metadata for refinement


> 4. Just to confirm, in the particle.star from M, the particle coordinates
> are defined in pixels by default. I will need to transfer them to CENTERed
> coordinates in tomograms before I use the matrix R, correct?


Because Warp only populates the rotational component you cannot use
matrices from the metadata we write out to calculate the 2D position in the
tilt series from the 3D position. The translational part is necessary to
calculate the 2D position from the 3D position and we do not provide it
because it is not necessary for refinement and we do not provide RELION
with tilt series.


> 5. In the tomogram.star from M, the rlnDefocusUV and rlnDefocusAngle
> columns describe the defocus of the center of each tilt image BEFORE any
> rotation (for example, the rotation for tilt axis), correct?
>

These are CTF parameters estimated for each 2D image, they are assumed to
correspond to the defocus at the center of the tomogram for that projection
image (and offsets are applied depending on the particle position relative
to the central plane)


> 6. After I transfer the 3D tomogram coordinates of each particle to 2D
> tilt series coordinates, how should I calculate the defocus of each
> particle? My understanding is that I should use the rlnDefocus described in
> step5, then consider the 2D coordinates and rlnTomoXTilt, YTilt, ZRot and
> XShiftAngst, YShiftAngst to calculate the defocus of each particle. But in
> this way, I will need to calculate these transformation parameters based on
> rlnTomoProjXYZW. Correct? Or is there a way to directly use the matrix R?
>

Copied the formula from the Warp paper below. The plane normal would be the
first three elements of the third column [a13, a23, a33] of the
rlnTomoProjX/Y/Z/W matrix.
[image: image.png]


> 7. In the tomogram.star from M, the rlnTomoVisibleFrames column describes
> which tilt images are included in the stacks. What is the order of those
> values in rlnTomoVisibleFrames? Based on tilt angle (from smallest to
> largest)? Or same as the tilt angle in warp_tiltseries/tiltseries.xml file?
> Or same as the tomostar/tiltseries.tomostar file? Or based on accumulated
> dose?


Yes, as sometimes a particle will end up outside the 2D tilt image for some
images in a tilt series. The order should be the same as the order of the
images in the tomograms.star file.

Hope that's helpful, have fun

Cheers,

Alister

> Thanks! I will try to dig into it and get back to the discussion later.
>

> Yunjie
>

> wrote:
>
>> There is no script, if that’s what you’re asking for. If you’d like to
>> write one and you have questions, I can answer specific questions.
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Dec 29, 2024, at 21:41, Yunjie Chang <changyj0821@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>> Hi Alister,
>>
>> Thanks for the quick response.
>>
>> Then is there any way to transfer the RELION5 tomo format star file to
>> single particle format? I'd like to have the defocus and position
>> information for each tilt image of all particles.
>>
>> Thanks
>> Yunjie
>>
>> On Mon, Dec 30, 2024 at 1:34 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Yunjie,
>>>
>>> This is not currently possible and support for exporting like this is
>>> not planned - if you have questions about how to interpret the RELION 5
>>> format I’m happy to help.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Alister
>>>
>>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>>
>>> On Dec 29, 2024, at 21:22, Yunjie Chang <changyj0821@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>> ﻿Hi Alister,
>>>
>>> I have a question about exporting particles by Warp. I checked the
>>> linked page in your previous reply to Juhua, but I want to do something
>>> different here.
>>>
>>> In the Windows M, there is an option to export particles after
>>> refinement and an option to choose export Volume or 2D particle stacks. If
>>> I choose the 2D particle stacks, M will generate the *.mrcs and the
>>> corresponding star file. This star file is in the RELION single particle
>>> format, with the defocus, position, Euler angles and other informations for
>>> each tilt image of every particle.
>>>
>>> However, in Linux Warp&M, if I use WarpTools ts_export_particles with
>>> --2d option, I still get the *.mrcs files for each particle, but the star
>>> file is in RELION5 tomo format. Is there any way to get the star file in
>>> RELION single particle format for Linux Warp&M?
>>>
>>> Thanks
>>> Yunjie
>>> On Tuesday, December 10, 2024 at 1:39:55 AM UTC+8 alist...@gmail.com
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Juha,
>>>>
>>>> c.f. https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/149
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Alister
>>>>
>>>> On Sat, 7 Dec 2024 at 01:24, Juha Huiskonen <juha.hu...@gmail.com>
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi all,
>>>>>
>>>>> I have refined particles with MCore and I want to classify them
>>>>> further in RELION. What is the correct way to do this?
>>>>>
>>>>> First, I tried using the particles.star file from
>>>>> M/species/particle/particles.star in WarpTools ts_export_particles. This
>>>>> STAR file is, however, not compatible.
>>>>>
>>>>> Should I use the STAR file created by RELION (the same one I used to
>>>>> create the species in M) instead? Does this workflow use the updated XML
>>>>> files for tilt series alignment? Are the updated particle alignment
>>>>> parameters in the M/species/particle/particles.star file relevant here?
>>>>>
>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>> Juha
>>>>>
